title: Update fetchSignals frontend types and connectivity test for structured result

requirements.summary: Update useFetchSignalsTest to handle structured fetchSignals success payload and update success toast messaging
acceptanceCriteria: `useFetchSignalsTest` compiles with the updated `fetchSignals` return type and no longer assumes `result.ok` is an array.
On success, the toast message is in English and references the structured payload (e.g., `statusCode` and/or `body`), without referencing a signals array length.

file_operations.description: Update the mutation success typing/handling to treat `result.ok` as a structured payload `{ statusCode; body; timestamp }` and adjust the success toast to reference e.g. `statusCode` and `body` length (English text only), removing any `signals.length` assumptions.

requirements.summary: Update generated frontend backend interface type definitions to reflect the new SignalFetchResult ok record shape
acceptanceCriteria: Frontend TypeScript builds without type errors related to `SignalFetchResult` or `actor.fetchSignals()`.
The frontend’s declared `SignalFetchResult` type matches the backend’s new `#ok` record shape (`statusCode`, `body`, `timestamp`) and the existing `#err : BotError` union.

file_operations.description: Regenerate or update the declared backend actor/types so `SignalFetchResult` is a discriminated union whose `ok` payload is a record containing `statusCode`, `body`, and `timestamp`, and `actor.fetchSignals()` returns that updated type.
